title: I am a Geologist, participating in my first Kaggle competition.
content: I have learnt a different way of looking at the problems. Initially I looked at it from a descriptive point of view and tried to solve them. Now I look at it from a more structural and deconstruct the bigger problem into smaller achievable parts.

comments.content:
We're glad to have you here Aditi.
We hope to read some approach (Geologist point-of-view code) from you till the end of the ROGII - Wellbore Geology Prediction.

Good luck,

Marília.

replies.content:
@mpwolke

Hello ! how's it going ?

Can you please share your inseights over this competition , specifically the data ?

Hi Ayush,

I've already share one Kaggle Notebook one month ago. When this competition started, at its very 1st day (as usual: )

Thank you for asking Khaire.

@mpwolke

This competition and mr . Market have one common trait .

Both are unpredictable .

In my opinion, this competition results might look very very different, it will for sure test ones design knowledge.
Hi @mpwolke, Many thanks for your comment. I will surely share my point-of-view as I keep working more on the problem. Apologies for the late response, I was a bit caught up at work.

comments.content: The geology background makes a lot of sense here. Reading stratigraphy trains you to extract signal from noisy, layered patterns, which is essentially what ARC grids demand. The shift from descriptive to structural thinking is also how most experienced competitors approach it. You got there faster than most. Would be great to see a write-up of how you frame the tasks visually.

replies.content:
Hi @marynaklo, Thanks a lot for the comment. When I started working on this problem, I had assumed that maybe everyone understands the concept of stratigraphy because in my mind I had seen and read it so many times that it was easy and normal for me. However, now as I approach the problem, meet with more people, I can begin to realise that it's a part of my domain knowledge, and also appreciate the multidisciplinary nature of the kaggle teams.

Are you a geologist too? How are you approaching this problem?

comments.content: Some wells have bad overlap, How does a decide in that case?

replies.content: Hi @shreygandhi, thanks for your comment. I haven't gone that far yet, but I will surely try to answer this if I figure it out.

comments.content:
@aditigeo

Hello ! Welcome to kaggle !

Can you please share your point of view about this competition and data ? As per my research till today the real life senerio is way different then what competition is presenting .

replies.content: Hi @ayushkhaire, Many thanks for your comment. I haven't gone that far in kaggle yet to comment on the comparison between the competition problem and real life scenario. I think kaggle is a platform for professionals and beginners with a wide range of expertise, so I guess the problem would be testing more on the ability to critically think and problem-solving approach which can be later used in real life scenarios. Even as a geologist when we get the training it's always in a more controlled environment with lesser parameters as compared to real life scenarios. Later we keep gathering experience, get feedbacks, work with several teams and gather the expertise to lead projects to solve real life scenarios.
